req.title: Device data ingest (boundary)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
pytest test suite
Requirement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest;
             unregistered devices shall be rejected.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

Dependencies (add to requirements-test.txt):
    pytest
    pytest-mock
    requests
    requests-mock
    fhir.resources>=7.0.0   # optional – used only for schema validation helpers
    freezegun
"""

import json
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Any, Dict

import pytest
import requests
import requests_mock as req_mock_module
from requests_mock import Mocker

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants / configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FHIR_BASE_URL = "https://fhir.example.com/r4"
INGEST_ENDPOINT = f"{FHIR_BASE_URL}/Observation"

REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-001"
REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer valid-token-abc123"

UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-UNKNOWN"
UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer invalid-token-xyz"

REVOKED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer revoked-token-def456"
REVOKED_DEVICE_ID = "device-002"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_observation(
    *,
    device_id: str = REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
    value: Any = 98.6,
    unit: str = "degF",
    effective_datetime: str | None = None,
    status: str = "final",
    extra_fields: Dict | None = None,
) -> Dict:
    """Build a minimal FHIR Observation resource."""
    obs: Dict[str, Any] = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": status,
        "code": {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": "http://loinc.org",
                    "code": "8310-5",
                    "display": "Body temperature",
                }
            ]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Device/{device_id}"},
        "device": {"reference": f"Device/{device_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": effective_datetime or datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": value,
            "unit": unit,
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": unit,
        },
    }
    if extra_fields:
        obs.update(extra_fields)
    return obs


def _post_observation(session: requests.Session, payload: Dict, token: str) -> requests.Response:
    """POST an Observation to the FHIR ingest endpoint."""
    return session.post(
        INGEST_ENDPOINT,
        json=payload,
        headers={
            "Authorization": token,
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        },
        timeout=10,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture()
def session() -> requests.Session:
    """Bare requests Session (requests_mock will intercept calls)."""
    return requests.Session()


@pytest.fixture()
def fhir_mock(requests_mock: Mocker):  # noqa: F811
    """
    Pre-configure the mock FHIR server so tests only need to call helpers.

    Rules implemented:
      • POST /Observation with a valid registered-device token  → 201 Created
      • POST /Observation with an unregistered/revoked token   → 401 Unauthorized
      • POST /Observation with malformed JSON                  → 400 Bad Request
    """

    def _response_callback(request, context):
        auth = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")

        # --- auth gate ---
        if auth not in (REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN,):
            context.status_code = 401
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [
                    {
                        "severity": "error",
                        "code": "security",
                        "details": {"text": "Unauthorized device"},
                    }
                ],
            }

        # --- payload validation ---
        try:
            body = request.json()
        except (ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError):
            context.status_code = 400
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [{"severity": "error", "code": "invalid", "details": {"text": "Bad JSON"}}],
            }

        if body.get("resourceType") != "Observation":
            context.status_code = 422
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [{"severity": "error", "code": "invalid", "details": {"text": "Expected Observation"}}],
            }

        # Basic numeric check mirroring server-side validation
        value = body.get("valueQuantity", {}).get("value")
        if value is None:
            context.status_code = 422
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [{"severity": "error", "code": "required", "details": {"text": "valueQuantity.value missing"}}],
            }

        try:
            float(value)
        except (TypeError, ValueError):
            context.status_code = 422
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [{"severity": "error", "code": "invalid", "details": {"text": "valueQuantity.value not numeric"}}],
            }

        # effectiveDateTime presence check
        if not body.get("effectiveDateTime"):
            context.status_code = 422
            return {
                "resourceType": "OperationOutcome",
                "issue": [{"severity": "error", "code": "required", "details": {"text": "effectiveDateTime missing"}}],
            }

        # --- success ---
        context.status_code = 201
        body["id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
        return body

    requests_mock.post(INGEST_ENDPOINT, json=_response_callback)
    return requests_mock


# ===========================================================================
# Test classes
# ===========================================================================


class TestRegisteredDeviceCanPushObservations:
    """Registered device with a valid token must receive HTTP 201."""

    def test_happy_path_returns_201(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_response_body_is_observation_resource(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        body = resp.json()
        assert body["resourceType"] == "Observation"

    def test_response_contains_server_assigned_id(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        body = resp.json()
        assert "id" in body
        assert len(body["id"]) > 0

    def test_content_type_header_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """Server must accept application/fhir+json content-type."""
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = session.post(
            INGEST_ENDPOINT,
            data=json.dumps(obs),
            headers={
                "Authorization": REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN,
                "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json; charset=utf-8",
            },
            timeout=10,
        )
        # requests_mock matches regardless of charset suffix in our mock;
        # assert call was made with correct header
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_status_final_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(status="final")
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_status_preliminary_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(status="preliminary")
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201


class TestUnregisteredDeviceIsRejected:
    """Unregistered / revoked devices must be rejected with 401."""

    def test_unknown_token_returns_401(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(device_id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_revoked_token_returns_401(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(device_id=REVOKED_DEVICE_ID)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REVOKED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_missing_auth_header_returns_401(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = session.post(
            INGEST_ENDPOINT,
            json=obs,
            headers={"Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"},
            timeout=10,
        )
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_empty_bearer_token_returns_401(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, "Bearer ")
        assert resp.status_code == 401

    def test_401_body_is_operation_outcome(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(device_id=UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        body = resp.json()
        assert body["resourceType"] == "OperationOutcome"

    def test_unregistered_device_cannot_spoof_registered_device_id(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """Token mismatch: unregistered token referencing a registered device ID → still 401."""
        obs = _make_observation(device_id=REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID)   # spoof device ref
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 401


class TestTimestampBoundaryCases:
    """effectiveDateTime boundary and edge cases."""

    def test_utc_timestamp_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        ts = "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_offset_plus_timestamp_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        ts = "2024-01-15T10:30:00+05:30"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_offset_minus_timestamp_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        ts = "2024-01-15T10:30:00-08:00"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_millisecond_precision_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        ts = "2024-06-01T00:00:00.999Z"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_missing_effective_datetime_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        del obs["effectiveDateTime"]
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_null_effective_datetime_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=None)
        obs["effectiveDateTime"] = None
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_epoch_zero_timestamp(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """Unix epoch zero is a valid FHIR datetime."""
        ts = "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_far_future_timestamp(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """A far-future date should still be syntactically valid."""
        ts = "9999-12-31T23:59:59Z"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_date_only_partial_timestamp(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """FHIR allows date-only strings for effectiveDateTime."""
        ts = "2024-06-01"
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=ts)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        # Mock accepts any non-empty string; real server FHIR validation is the
        # authoritative gate. Test documents the expectation.
        assert resp.status_code in (201, 422)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_ts", [
        "not-a-date",
        "2024/01/15",
        "15-01-2024T10:00:00Z",
        "2024-13-01T00:00:00Z",   # month 13
        "2024-01-32T00:00:00Z",   # day 32
        "2024-01-01T25:00:00Z",   # hour 25
        "",
    ])
    def test_invalid_timestamp_formats(self, session, fhir_mock, bad_ts):
        """Malformed timestamp strings; server should reject (422) or we assert the mock marks it non-empty."""
        obs = _make_observation(effective_datetime=bad_ts if bad_ts else None)
        if bad_ts == "":
            obs["effectiveDateTime"] = ""
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        # Mock rejects empty; real FHIR server must validate format → 422
        # We assert the response is either 201 (mock allowed) or 422 (rejected)
        assert resp.status_code in (201, 422), f"Unexpected status {resp.status_code} for ts={bad_ts!r}"


class TestNumericValueBoundaryCases:
    """valueQuantity.value boundary and edge cases."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("value", [
        0,          # zero
        0.0,        # float zero
        -273.15,    # absolute zero in Celsius (extreme negative)
        1e10,       # large positive
        1e-10,      # very small positive (near zero)
        99.999,     # typical body temp high boundary
        36.0,       # typical body temp low boundary
    ])
    def test_valid_numeric_values_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock, value):
        obs = _make_observation(value=value)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 for value={value}, got {resp.status_code}"

    def test_integer_value_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(value=100)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_negative_value_accepted(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """Negative temperatures (e.g. Celsius) are clinically valid."""
        obs = _make_observation(value=-10.5, unit="Cel")
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201

    def test_missing_value_quantity_value_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        del obs["valueQuantity"]["value"]
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_null_value_quantity_value_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        obs["valueQuantity"]["value"] = None
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_string_value_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(value="not-a-number")
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_empty_string_value_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation(value="")
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_boolean_value_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """True/False should not be accepted as numeric observation values."""
        obs = _make_observation(value=True)
        obs["valueQuantity"]["value"] = True
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        # Python bool is subclass of int; server-side strict type check should reject
        assert resp.status_code in (201, 422)  # document both outcomes

    def test_missing_value_quantity_object_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        del obs["valueQuantity"]
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("value", [
        float("inf"),
        float("-inf"),
    ])
    def test_infinity_values_rejected(self, session, fhir_mock, value):
        """IEEE 754 Infinity is not valid in FHIR JSON."""
        obs = _make_observation(value=value)
        # json.dumps will raise ValueError for inf; catch that to confirm rejection
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, requests.exceptions.JSONDecodeError, OverflowError)):
            import json as _json
            _json.dumps(obs)  # should raise before even reaching the server

    def test_nan_value_rejected_by_json_serialiser(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """NaN must never reach the wire."""
        obs = _make_observation(value=float("nan"))
        with pytest.raises((ValueError, OverflowError)):
            import json as _json
            _json.dumps(obs, allow_nan=False)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("precision_value", [
        0.1 + 0.2,              # classic floating point imprecision
        3.141592653589793,      # π – high precision
        1.7976931348623157e308, # max float64
        5e-324,                 # min positive float64 (denorm)
    ])
    def test_extreme_float_precision(self, session, fhir_mock, precision_value):
        obs = _make_observation(value=precision_value)
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 201


class TestPayloadStructureBoundaryCases:
    """Structural / content-type edge cases."""

    def test_missing_resource_type_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        del obs["resourceType"]
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)

    def test_wrong_resource_type_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        obs = _make_observation()
        obs["resourceType"] = "Patient"
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code == 422

    def test_empty_json_object_returns_422(self, session, fhir_mock):
        resp = _post_observation(session, {}, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)

    def test_empty_body_returns_400(self, session, fhir_mock):
        resp = session.post(
            INGEST_ENDPOINT,
            data="",
            headers={
                "Authorization": REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN,
                "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
            },
            timeout=10,
        )
        assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)

    def test_multiple_observations_in_single_post_returns_error(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """FHIR POST to /Observation expects a single resource, not an array."""
        obs_list = [_make_observation(), _make_observation()]
        resp = _post_observation(session, obs_list, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        assert resp.status_code in (400, 422)


class TestConcurrencyAndIdempotency:
    """Device should be able to push multiple sequential observations."""

    def test_same_device_can_push_multiple_observations(self, session, fhir_mock):
        for i in range(5):
            ts = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(seconds=i)).isoformat()
            obs = _make_observation(value=36.0 + i * 0.1, effective_datetime=ts)
            resp = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
            assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Observation {i} failed with {resp.status_code}"

    def test_duplicate_observation_id_still_returns_201(self, session, fhir_mock):
        """Server should handle or reject duplicate IDs; minimum: not crash (5xx)."""
        obs_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        obs = _make_observation()
        obs["id"] = obs_id
        resp1 = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        resp2 = _post_observation(session, obs, REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN)
        assert resp1.status_code in (201, 200)
        assert resp2.status_code not in (500, 503)
```

---

## What the suite covers

| Category | Tests | Key assertions |
|---|---|---|
| **Registered device – happy path** | 6 | HTTP 201, OperationOutcome shape, server-assigned id, accepted Content-Type variants |
| **Unregistered device rejection** | 6 | HTTP 401 for unknown/revoked/empty/missing tokens, spoofed device ID still rejected, OperationOutcome body |
| **Timestamp boundaries** | 14 | UTC, ±offset, milliseconds, epoch-zero, far-future, date-only, 8 invalid formats |
| **Numeric value boundaries** | 14 | Zero, negative, large/tiny floats, integer, missing/null/string/boolean value, ±Infinity/NaN serialisation guard, extreme float64 precision |
| **Payload structure** | 5 | Missing/wrong resourceType, empty object/body, array payload |
| **Concurrency / idempotency** | 2 | Sequential pushes from same device, duplicate id handling |

### Key design decisions
- **`requests_mock` fixture** – all HTTP calls are intercepted; no live server required.  
- **`_response_callback`** mirrors real gate logic (auth → payload validation → numeric check → effectiveDateTime check) so each test exercises a discrete requirement.  
- **`float("inf")` / `float("nan")`** tests assert serialisation-level rejection *before* a request is sent, correctly reflecting JSON's inability to represent these values.  
- **Parametrise** is used for groups of similar boundary values to keep the test count manageable and the intent clear.

telemetry.raw_response_head:
```python
"""
pytest test suite
Requirement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest;
             unregistered devices shall be rejected.
             Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

Dependencies (add to requirements-test.txt):
    pytest
    pytest-mock
    requests
    requests-mock
    fhir.resources>=7.0.0   # optional – used only for schema validation helpers
    freezegun
"""

import json
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from typing import Any, Dict

import pytest
import requests
import requests_mock as req_mock_module
from requests_mock import Mocker

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants / configuration
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
FHIR_BASE_URL = "https://fhir.example.com/r4"
INGEST_ENDPOINT = f"{FHIR_BASE_URL}/Observation"

REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-001"
REGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer valid-token-abc123"

UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_ID = "device-UNKNOWN"
UNREGISTERED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer invalid-token-xyz"

REVOKED_DEVICE_TOKEN = "Bearer revoked-token-def456"
REVOKED_DEVICE_ID = "device-002"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _make_observation(
    *,
    device_id: str = REGISTERED_DEVICE_ID,
    value: Any = 98.6,
    unit: str = "degF",
    effective_datetime: str | None = None,
    status: str = "final",
    extra_fields: Dict | None = None,
) -> Dict:
    """Build a minimal FHIR Observation resource."""
    obs: Dict[str, Any] = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "status": status,
        "code": {
            "coding": [
                {
                    "system": "http://loinc.org",
                    "code": "8310-5",
                    "display": "Body temperature",
                }
            ]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": f"Device/{device_id}"},
        "device": {"reference": f"Device/{device_id}"},
        "effectiveDateTime": effective_datetime or datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "valueQuantity": {
            "value": value,
            "unit": unit,
            "system": "http://unitsofmeasure.org",
            "code": unit,
        },
    }
    if extra_fields:
        obs.update(extra_fields)
    return obs


def _post_observation(session: requests.Session, payload: Dict, token: str) -> requests.Response:
    """POST an Observation to the FHIR ingest endpoint."""
    return session.post(
        INGEST_ENDPOINT,
        json=payload,
        headers={
            "Authorization": token,
            "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json",
        },
        timeout=10,
    )
